title: alchemilla arvensis subsp. microcarpa
intro: Alchemilla arvensis subsp. microcarpa, also known as the lesser lady's mantle, is a subspecies of the lady's mantle plant, which is a member of the Rosaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a popular remedy for treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Alchemilla arvensis subsp. microcarpa include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used topically to treat various skin conditions and promote overall skin health.
